name: hf_trackio
description: Track and visualize ML training experiments — metric logging, dashboards, HF Space sync

tools.name: hf_start_tracking
tools.description: Initialize experiment tracking for a project, optionally syncing to a HuggingFace Space.

project.description: Project name for grouping related experiments.

space_id.description: HuggingFace Space ID for persistent dashboard (e.g. 'username/my-tracking-space'). Required for remote/ephemeral training.

tools.name: hf_log_metrics
tools.description: Log metrics for the current training experiment.

metrics.description: Key-value pairs of metric names and values (e.g. {"loss": 0.42, "accuracy": 0.89}).

step.description: Training step or epoch number for this metric entry.

tools.name: hf_list_experiments
tools.description: List tracked experiments and their runs.

project.description: Filter by project name. If omitted, lists all projects.

knowledge:
You are operating the HuggingFace Trackio skill. This skill provides experiment tracking and visualization for ML training runs, with native HuggingFace Hub integration.

## Two Interfaces

Trackio has two complementary interfaces designed for different use cases:

### Python API (for logging during training)
Used inside training scripts to log metrics in real time:

```python
import trackio

# Initialize tracking
trackio.init(project="my-project", space_id="username/my-tracking")

# Log metrics at each step
for step in range(num_steps):
    loss = train_step()
    trackio.log({"loss": loss, "learning_rate": lr}, step=step)

# Finalize the run
trackio.finish()
```

Key functions:
- `trackio.init(project, space_id=None)` — Start a new tracking run. Always call first.
- `trackio.log(metrics_dict, step=None)` — Log a dictionary of metric values. Step is auto-incremented if omitted.
- `trackio.finish()` — Finalize the run. Always call at the end, even in error handlers.

### CLI (for retrieving and inspecting results)
Used to browse experiments, compare runs, and manage tracking data:

```bash
# List all projects
trackio list

# List runs in a project
trackio list --project my-project

# Get details of a specific run
trackio get <run_id>

# Show metrics chart in terminal
trackio show <run_id> --metric loss

# Sync local tracking data to HF Space
trackio sync --space-id username/my-tracking
```

Key CLI commands:
- `trackio list` — List projects and runs. Add `--json` for machine-readable output.
- `trackio get <run_id>` — Get full run metadata and final metrics. Add `--json` for structured output.
- `trackio show <run_id>` — Display metric curves. Use `--metric <name>` to filter.
- `trackio sync` — Push local tracking data to a HuggingFace Space for persistent access.

## CRITICAL: Remote Training Requires space_id

When training on ephemeral cloud infrastructure (HuggingFace jobs, cloud VMs, Colab), you MUST pass `space_id` when initializing tracking. Without it, metrics are stored locally on the ephemeral instance and are permanently lost when the instance terminates.

The space_id points to a HuggingFace Space that serves as a persistent dashboard. Metrics are streamed to this Space in real time, so you can monitor training progress from anywhere, even if the training instance crashes.

```python
# WRONG for cloud training - metrics lost when instance dies
trackio.init(project="my-training")

# CORRECT for cloud training - metrics persist to HF Space
trackio.init(project="my-training", space_id="username/training-dashboard")
```

For local training with persistent storage, space_id is optional but recommended for sharing results.

## TRL Integration

Trackio integrates natively with TRL (Transformer Reinforcement Learning) via the `report_to` training argument:

```python
from trl import SFTConfig

config = SFTConfig(
    output_dir="./output",
    report_to="trackio",
    # ... other training args
)
```

With this configuration, TRL automatically logs training loss, evaluation metrics, learning rate, and other training statistics to Trackio. No manual `trackio.log()` calls needed.

This is the recommended approach for all TRL-based training (SFT, DPO, GRPO). Always combine with `space_id` initialization for cloud training.

## Machine-Readable Output

All CLI commands support `--json` flag for programmatic consumption:

```bash
# Get structured run data
trackio get <run_id> --json | jq '.metrics.loss[-1]'

# List all runs as JSON array
trackio list --project my-project --json | jq '.[].run_id'
```

Use `--json` when parsing Trackio output in scripts or pipelines. The default human-readable output includes formatting that breaks automated parsing.

## Dashboard and Visualization

The HuggingFace Space dashboard provides:
- Real-time metric curves during training
- Run comparison (overlay multiple runs on the same chart)
- Hyperparameter tracking
- System metrics (GPU utilization, memory)
- Shareable URLs for collaboration

The dashboard updates automatically as new metrics arrive. No manual refresh needed.

## Common Pitfalls

- Forgetting `space_id` for cloud training — metrics permanently lost when instance terminates.
- Not calling `trackio.finish()` — run stays in 'running' state, metrics may not be fully flushed.
- Logging too frequently (every batch) on large datasets — creates excessive data. Log every N steps.
- Using human-readable CLI output in scripts — always use `--json` for programmatic access.
- Not initializing before logging — `trackio.log()` without `trackio.init()` silently drops metrics.
- Forgetting `report_to="trackio"` in TRL config — training runs without any tracking.
